channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Trump es el que no tiene las cartas sobre la mesa
item.description: La IA aumentará drásticamente el poder de los pequeños Estados y grupos en conflictos contra las grandes potencias.
item.dc:creator: Thomas L. Friedman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Foto Ilustración de Evan Hume para The New York Times

item.title: Una respuesta de 900 años para el dilema de la IA
item.description: Un sistema jurídico centenario podría definir la responsabilidad cuando la inteligencia artificial causa daño.
item.dc:creator: David French

item.title: En las faldas del monte Fuji, se libra una batalla contra los turistas revoltosos
item.description: Las enormes multitudes de visitantes durante la temporada de floración de los cerezos han puesto en vilo a una pintoresca ciudad japonesa. Algunos residentes intentan tomar las riendas.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández, Kiuko Notoya and Kentaro Takahashi

media:content.media:description: Turistas en abril en Fujiyoshida, Japón.

item.title: Antes los ricos no lucían así
item.description: La cirugía estética es un signo de éxito en el sentido más Kardashian: hazte rico y cómprate una cara.
item.dc:creator: Amy Odell

item.title: Today’s Spelling Bee Hints for May 3, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: A Love Letter to a Beautiful, Dying Thing
item.description: On May Day, we reflect on the sanctity of flowers.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Jackson

item.title: Esta investigación busca revertir el envejecimiento y podría cambiar a la humanidad
item.description: Una nueva terapia puede curar cientos de enfermedades e incluso revertir el envejecimiento.
item.dc:creator: Susan Dominus

item.title: Cómo controlar la adicción al teléfono
item.description: Contar los minutos es una mala forma de frenar los hábitos telefónicos. He aquí los verdaderos antídotos, según los expertos.
item.dc:creator: Brian X. Chen

item.title: Una IA facilitó a científicos instructivos para hacer armas biológicas
item.description: Algunos científicos compartieron transcripciones con el Times en las que los chatbots describían cómo ensamblar patógenos mortales y liberarlos en espacios públicos.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel J.X. Dance

item.title: Trump Shouldn’t Expect Much From His China Trip
item.description: China no longer looks to an erratic America as a model to emulate, but as a distraction to be managed.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Dreyer

item.title: With Iran, the U.S. Stands to Lose Its Reputation, Its Friends or Its Soul
item.description: President Trump has overextended the empire dangerously.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Caldwell
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Naila Ruechel for The New York Times

item.title: Los futbolistas profesionales que aún viven con sus padres
item.description: Julian Hall y Adri Mehmeti son dos adolescentes de Nueva York que están viviendo un sueño: aún no tienen edad para beber, pero se espera que le hagan frente a Messi.
item.dc:creator: David Waldstein and Vincent Alban

media:content.media:description: El objetivo de la mayoría de las jóvenes promesas estadounidenses es jugar en un club europeo.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Jordanians Struggle as Wars in the Mideast Slow Tourism to a Trickle
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Sunday, May 3, 2026.

item.title: Norway’s Northern Lights Nightmare
item.description: This Arctic region has seen a boom in tourism, but as tourists chase the northern lights, scammers chase their money.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel, Louise Krüger and Nicole Tung

media:content.media:description: A guided tour in February to see the northern lights in Grotfjorden, near Tromso, Norway.

item.title: How A.I. Is Transforming China’s Entertainment Industry
item.description: A.I.-generated microdramas have taken off. Celebrities have threatened legal action against the use of their likeness, while actors say jobs have dried up.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Wang and Jiawei Wang

item.title: Kathryn Stockett Has Finally Followed Up ‘The Help’
item.description: It was a blockbuster hit, yet she says she was “fired” by her publisher. After a spell in Bali, she’s back on home turf with “The Calamity Club.”
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Egan

media:content.media:credit: Thea Traff for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: “It’s impossible to write about a place like Mississippi, especially in the 1930s, and not talk racism and sexism,” Kathryn Stockett says of her new novel, “The Calamity Club.”

item.title: An Insurgency Threatens U.S. Mining Ambitions in Pakistan
item.description: Attacks by the Baloch Liberation Army could derail Pakistan’s plans for a billion-dollar mining deal with the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier, Zia ur-Rehman, Christiaan Triebert, Pablo Robles and Asim Hafeez

media:content.media:credit: Asim Hafeez for The New York Times

item.title: A Perilous Train Ride Home in Pakistan on the Jaffer Express
item.description: We witnessed crossfire while waiting for the Jaffer Express, a vital lifeline through Balochistan. For many, it’s the only way home.
item.dc:creator: Zia ur-Rehman and Asim Hafeez

media:content.media:credit: Asim Hafeez for The New York Times

item.title: A.I. Shakes Up China’s Entertainment Landscape
item.description: New A.I. tools are changing China’s entertainment industry and transforming one of its most popular genres: the microdrama.
item.dc:creator: Jiawei Wang, Ben Laffin and Rafaela Balster

item.title: Oil Tanker Hijacking Stokes Fear of Links Between Houthi Rebels and Somali Pirates
item.description: The attack’s location and timing fueled concerns about potential collaboration between Somalia’s pirates and Yemen’s Houthi rebels amid the Iran war.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar

media:content.media:description: Maritime police forces from Somalia’s semiautonomous Puntland region patrolling the Gulf of Aden last year.

item.title: Man Shot by ICE in California Indicted on Assault Charges
item.description: Prosecutors have accused the man of hitting federal agents with his vehicle as they tried to arrest him.
item.dc:creator: Orlando Mayorquín

media:content.media:credit: Andy Alfaro/Modesto Bee, via ZUMA Press Wire
media:content.media:description: Agents shot Carlos Ivan Mendoza Hernandez, 36, several times during a stop in Patterson, Calif., in April.

item.title: How Mamdani Would Change Grand Army Plaza for Walkers, Bikers and Drivers
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani's plan to connect the plaza to Prospect Park would expand pedestrian spaces, add bike lanes and reroute cars to improve traffic flow in the area.
item.dc:creator: Stefanos Chen and Helmuth Rosales

item.title: Corrections: May 3, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Sunday, May 3, 2026.

item.title: Trump Says He Is Reviewing Iran’s Latest Offer but Doubts It Is Acceptable
item.description: The comments came one day after Mr. Trump flatly rejected the proposal. He later clarified he was only briefed on the “concept of the deal.”
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tom Brenner for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump said Saturday he was reviewing Iran’s latest proposal but said he “can’t imagine that it would be acceptable.”

item.title: Delayed Louisiana Primaries Stoke Confusion at Ballot Box
item.description: On the first day of early voting, some glimpses of voter bewilderment came into focus, part of a dizzying scramble playing out days after a major Supreme Court ruling.
item.dc:creator: Emily Cochrane, Tim Balk, Nick Corasaniti and Aidan McCahill

media:content.media:credit: Emily Kask for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A ballot was cast during early voting at City Hall in Baton Rouge.

item.title: What Are the Odds?
item.description: Mark Diehl gambles on a return to the Sunday puzzle after a long absence.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:description: Solvers, don your robes.

item.title: Timmy the Stranded Whale Released Into the North Sea
item.description: After weeks of being stuck in shallow waters near Germany, Timmy, a humpback whale, was released into the North Sea on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga

media:content.media:description: Timmy the humpback whale, seen on April 29, 2026, during the effort to move it in a water-filled barge from the coast of Germany to the open sea.

item.title: Car With Explosive Devices Crashes Into Athletic Club in Portland, Ore.
item.description: A driver rammed his explosives-filled car into an athletic club Saturday. Hours later, devices were still being dismantled, with a bomb technician describing it “as the most complex scene that I’ve ever dealt with.”
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ziegler

media:content.media:description: A car had crashed through the front entrance of the Multnomah Athletic Club in Portland, Ore., and caught fire around 3 a.m., officials said.

item.title: Spirit Airlines Cancels All Flights, Stranding Passengers
item.description: The budget carrier abruptly canceled flights early on Saturday, leaving passengers to rush to make other plans. “Even if they go back into business, never again,” one traveler said.
item.dc:creator: Rylee Kirk

media:content.media:description: Some passengers were in the middle of their trips when they learned that their Spirit Airlines flights had been canceled.

item.title: Planning a World Cup Watch Party at a Bar? The ‘FIFA Police’ Are Lurking
item.description: Some businesses advertising watch parties in Canada’s two host cities are wary of running afoul of FIFA’s copyright, which protects advertising “World Cup” events.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai

media:content.media:description: Fans watching a FIFA World Cup match between Brazil and Cameroon at a bar in Toronto in 2014.

item.title: It Just Got Harder to Get an Abortion for Every Woman in America
item.description: A ruling by a federal appeals court has blocked access to abortion pills via telemedicine.
item.dc:creator: Jill Filipovic

media:content.media:credit: Aspen Mays for The New York Times

item.title: Spirit Airlines Shuts Down
item.description: After two stints in bankruptcy, Spirit Airlines — the low-cost carrier that reshaped American aviation but lost billions in recent years — canceled all flights and said it was “winding down all operations” on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Alisa Shodiyev Kaff

media:content.media:description: The Spirit Airlines ticket counter at Detroit Metropolitan Wayne County Airport in Romulus, Mich., was empty after the airline shut down overnight on Saturday.

item.title: It’s Only 5,000 Troops, but America Will Come to Regret Its Rash Withdrawal From Germany
item.description: This is more than just another bilateral spat.
item.dc:creator: Linas Kojala
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: The Head-Turning Hats of the 2026 Kentucky Derby
item.description: At the 152nd annual horse race at Churchill Downs, attendees trotted out some eye-catching ensembles.
item.dc:creator: The Styles Desk and Tierney L. Cross

item.title: Abortion Providers Forced to Adapt After Court Ruling Blocks Pill Access by Mail
item.description: The Fifth Circuit court’s ruling, which is being appealed, reinstates a requirement that patients visit a health care provider in person to obtain mifepristone, upending abortion access in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Astor, Chris Hippensteel and Pam Belluck
item.category: American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists

media:content.media:credit: Hannah Yoon for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: If the court’s temporary ruling stands, patients would have to make an in-person visit to obtain the abortion pill mifepristone.

item.title: Nicole Hollander, Acerbic Feminist Cartoonist, Dies at 86
item.description: For more than 30 years, she wrote and illustrated “Sylvia,” a comic strip about a tart-tongued, witty woman unafraid of expressing her many opinions.
item.dc:creator: Richard Sandomir

item.title: New Video Analysis Suggests Suspect in Correspondents’ Dinner Attack Fired First
item.description: A Times analysis of footage released by the F.B.I. on Thursday night appears to show the assailant shot at a Secret Service officer, who then returned fire.
item.dc:creator: Malachy Browne and McKinnon de Kuyper, Rafaela Balster, Alex Cardia and Monika Cvorak

item.title: Supreme Court Asked to Restore Access to Abortion Pill by Mail
item.description: A federal appeals court temporarily halted a Food and Drug Administration regulation that has greatly expanded access to the abortion pill mifepristone.
item.dc:creator: Ann E. Marimow and Pam Belluck

media:content.media:credit: Hannah Yoon for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Two manufacturers of the abortion pill mifepristone asked the Supreme Court on Saturday to immediately restore full access to the medication, putting the contentious issue of abortion back before the justices in an election year.

item.title: Steve Clark, Olympic Swimmer Who Confronted Depression, Dies at 82
item.description: A three-time gold medalist in the 1960s, he was a champion when athletes were expected to be stoic and vulnerability was viewed as a sign of mental weakness.
item.dc:creator: Jeré Longman

media:content.media:description: Steve Clark, at right, as a member of the American swim team at the 1964 Olympic Games. The others, from left, were Don Schollander, Gary Ilman and Mike Austin.

item.title: Gobernador mexicano acusado por EE. UU. pide licencia temporal
item.description: Fiscales estadounidenses acusaron a Rubén Rocha Moya de proteger al poderoso Cártel de Sinaloa a cambio de sobornos y apoyo político, lo que desató un escándalo nacional.
item.dc:creator: Jack Nicas

media:content.media:description: Rubén Rocha Moya, gobernador del estado mexicano de Sinaloa, dijo que había decidido tomar “una licencia temporal”.

item.title: En Sinaloa, la acusación de EE. UU. contra sus funcionarios no fue sorpresa
item.description: La imputación de Estados Unidos a un gobernador mexicano confirma lo que muchos sonorenses sospechaban hace tiempo: la línea entre el crimen organizado y las altas esferas del gobierno se ha difuminado.
item.dc:creator: Paulina Villegas

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Zehbrauskas para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Miembros de la Guardia Nacional Mexicana en Culiacán, Sinaloa, en febrero.

item.title: U.S. to Withdraw 5,000 Troops From Germany. But Thousands Will Remain.
item.description: The U.S. military has tens of thousands of personnel in Germany, more than in any other foreign country but Japan. Their significance extends well beyond Europe.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: Soldiers at the U.S. Army training facility in Hohenfels, Germany.

item.title: The Americans Who Could Be in Trump’s ‘Garden of Heroes’ Statue Park in D.C.
item.description: President Trump plans to build a park along the Potomac River featuring life-size statues of 250 Americans.
item.dc:creator: Adam Sella
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Huevos Enfrijolados Are Here for You
item.description: Rick Martínez’s easy recipe turns eggs, canned black beans and chipotles into a satisfying dinner (or breakfast or brunch or lunch).
item.dc:creator: Mia Leimkuhler

media:content.media:credit: Ghazalle Badiozamani for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Barrett Washburne.
media:content.media:description: Rick Martínez’s huevos enfrijolados.

item.title: U.S. Fast-Tracks Arms Deals Valued at $8.6 Billion to Mideast Partners
item.description: The Persian Gulf countries and Israel have faced repeated Iranian attacks during the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran. The State Department move bypassed congressional review.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman and Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Marco Rubio, the U.S. secretary of state, invoked an emergency provision to allow the “immediate sale” of weapons to Israel, Qatar, Kuwait and the United Arab Emirates.

item.title: 3 Medical Routines That Older People May Not Need
item.description: Some screenings and treatments no longer make sense for patients as they age. Researchers have just added a few more to the list.
item.dc:creator: Paula Span

item.title: May Monthly Bake: Extra-Tender Chocolate Chip Scones
item.dc:creator: Vaughn Vreeland

item.title: The Cannabis Industry’s New Best Friend? President Trump
item.description: The administration’s decision to relax federal regulations on medical marijuana comes with big tax breaks for many cannabis companies, and could drive new investment in the budding sector.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Southall

media:content.media:credit: Liam Kennedy for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Products at the LaFayette, N.Y., facility of Beak & Skiff, a 115-year-old apple orchard that makes a line of cannabis and hemp products called Ayrloom.

item.title: Slouching Toward Kamala Harris
item.description: The Democrats’ fundamental condition is a late-Trumpian stasis.
item.dc:creator: Ross Douthat

item.title: Who Will Stand Up to the Supreme Court Justices?
item.description: Congress must once again defend democracy from a hostile court.
item.dc:creator: Nikolas Bowie and Daphna Renan

media:content.media:credit: Photo illustration by Aleksey Kondratyev for The New York Times
